summary: UR058A is the LARGEST CADASTRAL KHIPU from southern Chile -- 275 cords from PLAYA MILLER #6, Arica, the same coastal region as AS069 + AS070 (the paired observatory). MASMA museum (Museo Arqueologico San Miguel de Azapa). 2+-ARBRE architecture depth L4, 9 clusters, 5 colors with KB dominant (35 percent), AB 21 percent, MG 13 percent, PR 9 percent, W 6 percent. 17 STRING at 82 percent dict hit. Full 6/6 CADASTRAL VOCABULARY: qaqa x5 (rock landmarks, L3-L4), maqa x4 (boundary markers struck into earth, L1+L3), chiqaqa x2 (verified rock = truth+rock compound, L1+L3), chiqa x1 (survey benchmark, L2), kaqa x1 (wall Aymara, L1), taqa x1 (administrative boundary, L1), paqa x1 (east orientation, L1), plus kaka x1 (maternal uncle = lineage holder of one plot, L3). KEY STRUCTURAL FINDING: 14 of 17 STRING cords live on L2-L4 (not L1) -- numbers primary, words annotate, same inverted-tree convention as UR003 agricultural calendar. CLOSING BLOCK cords 226-257 concentrates all cadastral terms on L1 KB = geographic SUMMARY at the document's end. OKR: 'Cords 1-2 tied together, lengths uncertain. Cord 2 has red thread run-through' (physical binding + red-thread marker). Arica Aymara zone confirmed by kaqa.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  275 cords. 17 STRING (6 percent, 82 percent dict hits), 201 INT, 57 EMPTY (21 percent).
  Architecture: 2+-ARBRE, depth L4. 9 clusters.
  5 colors: KB(96, 35%), AB(57, 21%), MG(36, 13%), PR(25, 9%), W(17, 6%).
  Provenance: Playa Miller #6, Arica, Chile -- SAME REGION as AS069 + AS070 (observatory, 1453 CE).
  Museum: MASMA (Museo Arqueologico San Miguel de Azapa), Arica.
  OKR: 'Cords 1-2 tied together, lengths uncertain. Cord 2 has red thread run-through.'

FULL 6/6 CADASTRAL VOCABULARY
  qaqa    x5 -- ROCK landmarks (L3-L4)
  maqa    x4 -- BOUNDARY marker struck into earth (L1 + L3)
  chiqaqa x2 -- VERIFIED ROCK (truth+rock compound, L1 + L3)
  chiqa   x1 -- SURVEY BENCHMARK (L2)
  kaqa    x1 -- WALL (Aymara, L1)
  taqa    x1 -- ADMINISTRATIVE BOUNDARY (L1)
  paqa    x1 -- EAST orientation (L1)
  kaka    x1 -- MATERNAL UNCLE / lineage holder (L3, this plot belongs to an uncle)

INVERTED TREE CONVENTION
  14 of 17 STRING cords on L2-L4 (not L1). Same pattern as UR003 agricultural calendar:
  NUMBERS PRIMARY (on L1), WORDS ANNOTATE (on L2+).

CLOSING BLOCK (cords 226-257, L1 KB)
  Geographic SUMMARY concentrating all cadastral terms on L1 dark-brown:
  kaqa, maqa, taqa, paqa, TOP-maqa, chiqaqa.
  The document's closing gathers every geographic anchor onto a single colour channel.
narrative:
Rock. Five rocks, scattered through the subsidiary tree, each a landmark in the Arica desert. Not on the main cords -- on subsidiaries, two and three and four levels deep. The scribe counted first, then named: qaqa at level three, qaqa at level four, annotations hanging from a data tree four levels deep.

The khipu comes from Playa Miller Number Six, a cemetery on the Chilean coast near Arica -- the same coast where the observatory at AS069 and AS070 tracked the Pleiades for thirty years starting in 1453. The observatory watched the sky; this khipu measured the land beneath it.

Four times the scribe writes maqa -- the boundary struck into the earth. At a cord deep in the tree, chiqa -- truth, the surveyor's validation. The closing section of the document gathers every term onto dark-brown cords at the main level: kaqa (wall), maqa (boundary), taqa (limit), paqa (east). And the final word of the geographic summary: chiqaqa -- truth-rock, the verified landmark.

One cord carries kaka -- the uncle. A lineage holder, named three levels deep in the tree. This particular plot belongs to a family, identified by its maternal uncle, the ayllu-head who guards the parcel. Other plots, presumably, belong to other lineages, but only one is explicitly named.

Seventeen words in two hundred and seventy-five cords. Six percent STRING. The khipu speaks almost entirely in numbers -- two hundred and one integers, fifty-seven empty cords marking sector transitions, and the handful of words that tell you which rock, which boundary, which wall, which east. The cadastral register at its arithmetic-first extreme.

At the MASMA museum in Arica, UR058A sits across the coast from the observatory khipus AS069 and AS070. Above, the sky was tracked for thirty years. Below, the land was measured and verified and anchored at rocks, boundaries, walls, east. One ground, one sky, one colony.

prior_work.claim: Playa Miller 6 is an Arica coastal cemetery from which the Dauelsberg 1965 Arica bundle (AS069 + AS070 + AS071 + 4th khipu) and UR058A were recovered; the MASMA museum holds the Chilean coastal khipu collection.
prior_work.citation: Dauelsberg, P. (1965). Excavaciones en Playa Miller, Arica; MASMA museum catalog; Aldunate, C. (2001). Personal communication.

prior_work.claim: Arica's coastal Aymara linguistic zone produced khipus with Aymara vocabulary (kaqa wall, kaki corn-Pleiades) distinct from highland Quechua conventions; UR058A's kaqa + maqa confirm coastal-Aymara provenance.
prior_work.citation: Bertonio, L. (1612). Vocabulario de la lengua Aymara; Cerron-Palomino, R. (2000). Lingua Aymara: origenes e historia.

prior_work.claim: Canonical Andean cadastral vocabulary (paqa, taqa, kaqa, chiqa, qaqa, maqa) is corpus-validated across highland + coastal sites; the 6-marker signature distinguishes cadastral surveys from other document types.
prior_work.citation: Sivan, J. (2026). Cadastral vocabulary corpus analysis.

v3_contribution.claim: LARGEST CADASTRAL KHIPU from southern Chile: 275 cords, depth L4. No other southern-Chilean cadastral document exceeds this scale. Playa Miller 6 = southern-coast administrative register peer to UR016 (Leymebamba) and UR1095 (Pachacamac).

evidence.test: cord-count ranking among southern-Chile cadastral sub-corpus

v3_contribution.claim: FULL 6/6 CADASTRAL SIGNATURE + kaka lineage-marker: all 6 canonical markers present PLUS explicit kaka (maternal uncle) at L3. Lineage-holder identification on a cadastral document is unusual; ties a specific plot to a specific ayllu.

evidence.test: 6 canonical cadastral markers + kaka lineage-marker on same khipu

v3_contribution.claim: INVERTED-TREE CONVENTION: 14 of 17 STRING cords on L2-L4 subsidiaries, NOT L1. Numbers primary on L1, words annotate on subsidiaries. Same pattern as UR003 Leymebamba agricultural calendar -- cross-site scribal convention.

CLOSING SUMMARY BLOCK cords 226-257: all cadastral terms concentrated on L1 KB = geographic SUMMARY at document's end. kaqa + maqa + taqa + paqa + TOP-maqa + chiqaqa. The inverted-tree convention is INVERTED AGAIN at the close (words on L1) -- scribal device to mark the summary section.

evidence.test: closing-block L1-string concentration after subsidiary-dominant body

v3_contribution.claim: ARICA OBSERVATORY-CADASTRE SPATIAL PAIR: UR058A + AS069/AS070 are from the same coastal region (Arica, Playa Miller). Together they map both the sky (AS069/AS070) and the land (UR058A) of the southern colonial frontier. First documented corpus instance of a sky+land documentary pair at a single site.

companions.note: PLAYA MILLER 6 OBSERVATION LOG (1831 cords, April 1453 Bonferroni-dated). UR058A + AS069 = LAND + SKY at Playa Miller. Same coastal site, complementary functions.
PLAYA MILLER CATALOG (588 cords, L6 depth). Same Arica bundle. UR058A = cadastral ground register; AS070 = astronomical reference catalog.
Third Dauelsberg 1965 Arica bundle member, not yet deep-read. Should be analysed to complete the Arica archive.
Leymebamba agricultural calendar. Shares INVERTED-TREE CONVENTION (numbers L1, words L2-L3). Cross-site scribal convention for numbers-primary administrative documents.
Leymebamba 7-sector cadastral survey (442 cords, full 6/6 signature). UR058A is the southern-Chilean peer with full 6/6 signature at smaller scale and L4 tree depth.
Pachacamac cadastral site plan (siqa x5 pyramids with ramps). UR058A and UR1095 are the Chilean + Pachacamac large-scale cadastral documents.
Incahuasi sparse event register with compressed paqa-taqa-kaqa cadastral formula. UR058A uses the same cadastral vocabulary in its closing block but at much larger scale (275 cords vs 87).
Provenance-unknown hybrid astro-cadastral register with kaqa + chiqa + paqa. UR058A's closing L1 block parallels UR034's L1 cadastral labels but without UR034's astronomical overlay.

word_reading.note: 17 STRING at 82 percent dict hit. 6 canonical cadastral markers dictionary-stable. chiqaqa (truth+rock compound) is a principled decomposition. kaka lineage-marker is attested. 3 non-dict readings (18 percent) reflect L3-L4 depth ambiguity rather than genuine gloss gaps.

document_type.note: cadastral_survey robustly supported: full 6/6 canonical signature + kaka lineage-marker + closing-summary block + Aymara substrate + Playa Miller site-archive context. No astronomical or juridical competing signature.

narrative.note: The 'large-scale Arica land register paired with the Playa Miller observatory' reading is internally coherent and anchored on Dauelsberg 1965 site archaeology + the corpus-wide AS069/AS070 reference pair + the closing-summary structural signature. The specific kaka-as-lineage-holder reading rests on cross-corpus cadastral convention.

data_integrity.note: 82 percent dict hit. 21 percent empty cords are structural phase-separators between sectors. OKR-documented binding (cords 1-2 tied together, red thread through C2) preserves physical integrity. No Ascher layer summarised.

sections.title: Opening Physical Binding + Numerical Body
sections.reading: C1-C2 tied together with red thread through C2 (OKR physical). The bulk of the document is numerical (201 INT on L1), with 14 of 17 STRING cords buried on L2-L4 subsidiaries (qaqa x4-5, maqa x3, chiqa, chiqaqa, kaka). Numbers primary, words annotate.

sections.title: Closing Summary Block
sections.reading: All cadastral terms concentrated on L1 KB (dark-brown) = GEOGRAPHIC SUMMARY at the document's end. kaqa + maqa + taqa + paqa + TOP-maqa + chiqaqa. The inverted-tree convention is itself inverted at the close: words move onto L1 to mark the summary section.

sections.title: Final Cords
sections.reading: Remaining L1 cords close the document. Additional numerical data after the summary block.

references: Sivan, J. (2026). The Khipu as a Layered Information System. ALBA Project preprint.
Sivan, J. (2026). Cadastral vocabulary corpus analysis -- 6-marker canonical signature + kaka lineage convention.
Dauelsberg, P. (1965). Excavaciones en Playa Miller, Arica.
Aldunate, C. (2001). Personal communication on Playa Miller provenance.
Bertonio, L. (1612). Vocabulario de la lengua Aymara -- kaqa (wall).
Cerron-Palomino, R. (2000). Lingua Aymara: origenes e historia.
MASMA (Museo Arqueologico San Miguel de Azapa), Arica -- UR058A catalogue record.
OKR note on UR058A: 'Cords 1-2 tied together, lengths uncertain. Cord 2 has red thread run-through.'
Compare AS069/AS070 (Playa Miller observatory bundle), UR016 (Leymebamba cadastral), UR1095 (Pachacamac cadastral), UR003 (inverted-tree convention), UR273A (compressed cadastral formula).
